content: Does anyone know another source for prebuilt busybox binaries? Usually I grab x64 from https://busybox.net/downloads/binaries/ but it doesn't seem like there is anything for aarch64
If I can even get this thing built, keep getting segfaults within make...

In https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/ci/blob/master/Dockerfile we built busybox for the CI. I don't recall issues cross-building it, so it may be related to the config (since I used a trimmed one) or the environment.
Oh awesome, thanks for the link. I keep going through config and turning things off, not sure why this is being such a problem for me
Buildroot always looks quite ergonomic but I’ve never tried it. Do you use it to build the kernel itself or just utils?

I used to provide my own kernel and config. I used it for kernel development, booting a VM. Not embedded stuff. The workflow was too slow though, now I just use a Debian root file system because it’s easier.
I’m doing the same, I provide my own kernel and config and it does all the busybox stuff. Having only used it for x86, I have no idea about aarch64.
I’ve just used a build script that builds then boots with the downloaded busybox. But also never had a use for anything other than x86
Does anybody build on an aarch64 machine? Haven’t dug too much into it but I was getting segfaults just building
I like to use those containers when cross compiling. Probably you can also use them to build busybox. May be worthwile tyring some compilers and an enviroment different from your host.
I had a full VM spun up but trying docker is a good idea, thanks for the containers link
